excerpt: Strive against your selves with hunger and thirst, for its spiritual reward is similar to that of striving in battle against the unbelievers
source_location_hint: Islamic tradition on asceticism and hunger

excerpt: Whoever fills his stomach will not be admitted into the heavenly kingdom
source_location_hint: Teachings on satiety and the afterlife

excerpt: He who eats little, laughs little, and is content with breeches
The lord and the leader of all acts is hunger
source_location_hint: Centrality of hunger in spiritual discipline

excerpt: Eat and drink until the belly is half-full for that is a portion of prophet hood
source_location_hint: Moderation in eating as prophetic practice

excerpt: Allah Most High boasts to the angels of the person who eats little...For every bite that he has passed up, I shall give him a rank in heaven
source_location_hint: Divine reward for fasting and abstinence

excerpt: Do not kill your souls with large quantities of food and drink, for the soul is like a sowing ground; if it is over-watered, the plants will die
source_location_hint: Harm of overeating to the soul

excerpt: A man fills no container worse than his stomach...one third of the stomach is for food, one third for drink, and one third for breath
source_authority: Islamic tradition (attributed to al-Miqdam ibn Madikarib)

excerpt: Keep yourselves hungry and naked so that it enables your souls to see Allah
Satan flows inside the body of a person like blood in the veins. Make his passage difficult with hunger
The believer eats with one intestine; the hypocrite with seven
source_location_hint: Distinction between belief and hypocrisy in eating habits

excerpt: Continually knock on the door of heaven until your door is opened...Hunger and thirst
Keep your belch at a distance, for whoever is more satisfied in this world will be more hungry in the next
source_location_hint: Warning against satiety in this world

excerpt: The prophets of highest resolve, my brethren who have preceded me, have obtained grace from Allah Most High...I fear that if I enjoy comfort and ease, my rank will be less than theirs
source_location_hint: Prophet's preference for hunger to maintain rank with predecessors

excerpt: Ahley bait never ate bread made of wheat for three days in a row during the time of the Messenger
source_author: Abu Hurairah (may God be pleased with him)
source_location_hint: Ascetic practice of the Prophet's Household

excerpt: I prefer to eat one morsel less of the evening meal, than to perform formal prayer throughout the night until morning
source_author: Abu Sulayman al-Darani (may God be pleased with him)

excerpt: What are you afraid of? Are you afraid that you will remain hungry? What an idea! that Allah Most High would give hunger to Muhammad PBUH and his Companions, and withhold it from you and the likes of you
source_author: Fudayl (may God be pleased with him)
source_location_hint: Trust in divine provision and hunger

excerpt: O Lord! You keep me hungry! You keep me in Your company in long nights of seclusion. How have I achieved this station with You? For you did not do this with anyone but your friends
source_author: Kahmas (may God be pleased with him)
source_location_hint: Hunger as sign of divine friendship

excerpt: Blessed is the person who possesses enough grain to suffice his needs and be independent of people
source_author: Malik al-Dinar (may God be pleased with him)

excerpt: Blessed is the person who is hungry in the morning, and hungry in the evening and with whom Allah Most High is pleased
source_author: Muhammad al-Wasi (may God be pleased with him)

excerpt: The saints and the wise have searched and found nothing more beneficial in religion and the world than hunger, and have found nothing more harmful to the Hereafter than satiety
source_author: Sahl al-Tustari (may God be pleased with him)

excerpt: Allah Most High has not befriended any person except through hunger. No one has walked upon water except through hunger. No one has rolled up the earth so as to travel a hundred parasangs in a night except through hunger
source_author: Abd al-Wahid Zayd (may God be pleased with him)
source_location_hint: Hunger as foundation of miracles and divine friendship

excerpt: Moosa (A), during those forty days when Allah Most High wished to talk with him, ate nothing
The worst of my people are those whose bodies have been planted upon the comforts of this world. All their aspirations are for different kinds of food and different kinds of clothing
O Moses, know that your residence is the grave. You must restrain your body from too many carnal appetites
Whoever desires something, buys it, and then abstains from it for the sake of Allah Most High will be forgiven
source_author: Prophet Muhammad (S), transmitted by Ibn Umar via Nafi

excerpt: Utbah al-Ghulam would place dough in the sun until it was dry, before he ate it. He would not bake it in order to abstain from its good taste
source_work: Sayings and practices of the Saints
source_author: Utbah al-Ghulam (may God be pleased with him)
source_location_hint: Deliberate avoidance of taste pleasure in food

excerpt: Malik Dinar desired milk for forty years, but never drank it. Someone brought fresh dates to him...You eat them for I have not eaten any for forty years
source_author: Malik al-Dinar (may God be pleased with him)

excerpt: My master used to wish for hot bread...He took one bite then put it aside and began to weep. He said: O Lord Allah, You have placed my desire before me, but will you punish me?
source_author: Ahmad ibn Abi al-Hawari, reporting Abu Sulayman al-Darani

excerpt: I was walking through the market of Basrah. I saw very fine leeks. The appetite for them leaped inside me. I swore an oath not to eat them until that time when I reach Allah
source_author: Malik ibn Daygham (may God be pleased with him)

excerpt: It has been fifty years since I have divorced myself from the world in the desire for a single draught of milk. I have not drunk that and I shall not drink it until that time when I reach God
source_author: Malik al-Dinar (may God be pleased with him)
source_location_hint: Fifty-year refusal of a desired substance

excerpt: I arrived at the door of Dawud Tayi and heard his voice. He was saying: Once you desired a carrot and I gave it to you. Now you want dates? You will never get them
source_author: Hammad (may God be pleased with him), reporting Dawud al-Tai
source_location_hint: Spiritual discipline of denying bodily desires

excerpt: Enliven your souls by laughing little; cleanse them with hunger until they become finely pure
source_location_hint: Hunger as purification of the soul

excerpt: Whoever stays hungry, becomes astute and his thoughts become great
There was never a day when I sat hungry for the sake of Allah, that I did not receive a new wisdom from Him in my soul
source_author: Shibli (may God be pleased with him)
source_location_hint: Hunger as gateway to divine wisdom

excerpt: Do not fill yourselves with food, for it will extinguish the light of spiritual knowledge in your souls
source_location_hint: Satiety as obstacle to spiritual knowledge

excerpt: Continue to knock at the door of Paradise with hunger, until it opens
source_location_hint: Hunger as persistent petition to heaven

excerpt: Each one of you has placed a food bag crammed with provisions between yourself and Allah Most High. After that you desire to find the pleasure of conversation with Allah? This will never happen
source_author: Junayd al-Baghdadi (may God be pleased with him)
source_location_hint: Food as barrier between soul and divine intimacy

excerpt: I do not want it! I will replace it with a day of satiety and a day of hunger (fast). When I become hungry, I will be patient and when I am sated, I will give thanks
source_location_hint: David's fast (Saum-i Dawood) as model of balance

excerpt: You possess the treasures of this world. Then why do you remain hungry?...I fear that if I eat my fill, I shall forget the hunger of the poor
All sins arise from the carnal appetites and all carnal appetites arise from satiety
source_location_hint: Root causation of sin in satiety

excerpt: I never ate my fill. Because of that, I have not sinned, nor have I intended to sin
source_author: Dhu al-Nun al-Misri (may God be pleased with him)

excerpt: The first innovation that appeared after the Messenger was satiating oneself. Because the people eat their fill, their souls became disobedient
source_author: ʿAʾisha (may God be pleased with her)

excerpt: Hunger is a jewel in the treasures of Allah Most High. He gives it to those who love Him, but He does not give it to everyone
source_location_hint: Hunger as divine gift and distinction

excerpt: Allah Most High removes the thought of women from the soul of every disciple who eats dry bread for a year and eats half of that amount to which he is accustomed
O disciples! Do not eat much of your bread, for then you will drink too much water, and then you will sleep too much; then you will experience much grief at the Resurrection
source_location_hint: Chain causation: eating, drinking, sleeping, regret

excerpt: Nocturnal pollution is a punishment, that is caused by satiety
source_author: Abu Sulayman al-Darani (may God be pleased with him)

excerpt: Every breath is a jewel and it is the capital of human being. Wasting it unnecessarily is foolishness
I saw Ali Gurgani throwing barley meal into his mouth...Between this and my eating bread there is a difference of seventy glorifications of Allah...I have not eaten bread for forty years
source_author: Sari al-Saqati (may God be pleased with him), reporting Ali al-Gurgani

excerpt: Six things occur to whoever eats his fill: 1) He does not obtain the sweetness of worship 2) His desire and memory for learning wisdom weakens 3) He is deprived of compassion for others 4) Worship becomes troublesome for him 5) His carnal appetites are increased 6) While believers are going around the mosques, he is going around privies
source_author: Abu Sulayman al-Darani (may God be pleased with him)

excerpt: There is nothing in this world that contains only benefits, and not a single harm, except in eating sparingly
source_location_hint: Unique universal benefit of dietary moderation

excerpt: Fast, so that you remain healthy
Ibrahim Adham was once asked the price of something in a brother's possession. He said: It is expensive! To which he said: Make it cheap by abandoning it
source_author: Ibrahim al-Adham (may God be pleased with him)

excerpt: The destination of whatever enters the stomach is foul, while the destination of whatever is given in charity is the pure treasures of Allah Most High

The Messenger looked at someone who had a fat belly and pointing to it said: If this was not where you have put it, and had you put it some place it, it would have been better for you in voluntaly charity for the sake of Allah Most High
source_location_hint: Wealth for charity better than food consumption
